output.summary: Implemented two changes from planning: (1) Unified per-page query variable from post-type-specific names to single 'show_per_page' across PHP, JS, and query vars registration. (2) Fixed dropdown chevron arrows by adding !important to all background sub-properties to override the global bg-none! from forms.css.

fixesApplied.description: Changed per-page query var from post-type-specific to unified 'show_per_page'
changes: Line 119: Updated comment from 'Per-page from URL (namespaced by post type)' to 'Per-page from URL (unified query var)'
The select name attribute on line 269 already uses $per_page_var, so it automatically picks up the new unified value
fixesApplied.impact: URLs will now use ?show_per_page=20 instead of ?per_page_post=20, ?per_page_team_member=20, etc. This unifies the parameter across all archive post types.

fixesApplied.description: Changed per-page query var from post-type-specific to unified 'show_per_page'
changes: Line 21: Changed 'var perPageVar = 'per_page_' + postType;' to 'var perPageVar = 'show_per_page';'
This affects loadFiltersFromUrl (line 316) which now looks for 'show_per_page' in URL params
The serializeForm function automatically serializes the select with name='show_per_page' correctly
fixesApplied.impact: JavaScript now reads and writes the unified 'show_per_page' parameter for all archive types.

fixesApplied.description: Replaced five post-type-specific per_page vars with single 'show_per_page'
changes: This ensures WordPress recognizes 'show_per_page' as valid for get_query_var() retrieval
fixesApplied.impact: WordPress will now accept and process ?show_per_page=X in archive URLs across all post types.

fixesApplied.description: Fixed dropdown chevron arrows by adding !important to all background sub-properties
changes: Lines 126-133: Updated .archive select rule with comprehensive fix
Updated comment to explain root cause: global forms.css '@apply bg-none!' compiles to 'background: none !important' which overrides all background sub-properties
fixesApplied.rationale: The global forms.css uses '@apply bg-none!' (line 128) which compiles to 'background: none !important'. The CSS 'background' shorthand resets ALL background sub-properties (image, repeat, position, size) with !important. This overrides both the global arrow definition and the archive-specific override. Adding !important to all four sub-properties allows them to compete with the global !important.
fixesApplied.impact: Dropdown arrows will now display correctly on all <select> elements within .archive context (filter dropdowns, per-page selector).

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - informational only, no functional impact

syncStatus.notes: PHP and JS both updated with matching 'show_per_page' variable. Query vars registration updated to match. CSS fix is isolated to Archive.css with proper scoping.

testPage.note: Test page already has 11 Archive variations from previous revisions. No test page changes needed for this fix.

notes: Query var unification: URLs now use ?show_per_page=20 instead of post-type-specific parameters
Backward compatibility: Old bookmarked URLs with per_page_* params will be ignored (no errors), falling back to default postsPerPage from block attributes
Dropdown arrow fix: All four background sub-properties need !important because the global 'background: none !important' shorthand sets all of them with !important

The global forms.css bg-none! issue is a pre-existing bug that affects all selects theme-wide. The archive-specific fix is scoped to .archive select only to avoid side effects. A separate task should address the global issue.
CSS specificity: The .archive select rule with !important overrides the global forms.css bg-none! for this specific context
No TypeScript changes needed - this is a PHP/JS/CSS-only fix
Build passes without errors, ready for QA verification

functionalChecks: Change per-page selector from 9 to 18 - verify URL updates to ?show_per_page=18 (not ?per_page_post=18)
Test on different archive variations (team_member, resource, policy) - all should use ?show_per_page=X
Verify dropdown arrows are visible on all <select> elements (filter dropdowns, per-page selector)
Test browser back/forward buttons - per-page state should persist via unified query var
Verify AJAX filtering updates URL with show_per_page parameter correctly
visualChecks: Inspect filter dropdowns - white chevron arrow should be visible at right edge
Inspect per-page selector - white chevron arrow should be visible
Arrows should be positioned 16px from right edge, vertically centered
